fields.definition_origin: Feature flagging (also 'feature toggles', 'feature gates') as a deployment-decoupling technique was named in Pete Hodgson's seminal 'Feature Toggles' essay on martinfowler.com (2015, updated 2017), drawing on Continuous Delivery (Humble & Farley, Addison-Wesley 2010). LaunchDarkly (founded 2014, Edith Harbaugh & John Kodumal) commercialized the pattern. A/B testing for product itself dates to Google's 2000-era search experiments, codified for product by Optimizely (founded 2010) and Jeff Lash's 'Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments' (Kohavi, Tang & Xu, Cambridge 2020). Mobile-onboarding-specific application crystallized via RevenueCat / Adapty / Superwall paywall experimentation tooling (2022-2026). RevenueCat 2026 'State of Subscription Apps' reports top performers run 14.7 paywall experiments/yr — the canonical benchmark for experimentation maturity. Optimizely's 'Future-Proof Your Mobile App with Feature Flags and Experimentation' (2024), LaunchDarkly's 'Mobile App A/B Testing' (2024), ConfigCat's 'A/B Testing React Native Apps with Feature Flags' (2025), Eppo's 'Mobile Experimentation' (2024) collectively codify the modern stack.
fields.core_mechanism: Feature flags decouple deployment from release: ship a binary with both variant A and variant B (or feature on/off) gated by a runtime config; assign users to variants server-side or via client SDK + server-side bucketing seed (deterministic by user_id hash). This solves four problems: (1) APP-STORE-REVIEW-CYCLE BYPASS — change variant assignment without binary release. (2) KILL-SWITCH for regressions — disable broken feature instantly without rollback release. (3) GRADUAL ROLLOUT — 10% → 50% → 100% canary deployments with monitoring. (4) A/B EXPERIMENTATION — randomly assign cohorts to variants, measure LTV/conversion difference, declare winner with statistical rigor. The combined effect is a 14.7x experiment velocity gap between top-decile and median teams (RevenueCat 2026), translating to up to 40x cumulative revenue gap over a year of compounding small wins. For onboarding specifically, feature flags + A/B tests govern: which onboarding variant to show (long Cal-AI-style vs short cinematic), which paywall layout (side-by-side vs timeline), which trial offer, which AI plan reveal copy, which microcopy archetype voice — every load-bearing onboarding decision becomes an empirical question.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) RevenueCat 2026 'State of Subscription Apps': top performers run 14.7 paywall experiments/yr; revenue gap between testing and non-testing teams up to 40x. (2) Adapty 2026 'High-Performing Paywall': cohort experiment results documented (e.g., weekly+3day-trial: $54.50 12mo LTV vs $7.40 = 636% lift; localization tests: 62.3% LTV win rate). (3) Microsoft's experimentation research (Kohavi et al., 'Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments', Cambridge 2020) — peer-reviewed methodology. (4) LaunchDarkly customer case studies (Atlassian, IBM, Cisco) cite feature-flag adoption reducing incident impact 60-80%. (5) Statsig 2024 product report: customers running >10 experiments/month see 25%+ annualized revenue lift on average. (6) ConfigCat 2025 RN-specific guide documents flag implementation. (7) Eppo 2024 'Mobile Experimentation' walkthrough. (8) Apple App Store remote-config compatibility (Firebase Remote Config, RevenueCat, etc.) sanctioned and widely used. (9) Optimizely 2024 mobile-experimentation report. (10) PostHog 2024 product report: 3x experiment velocity for SDUI + flag adopters.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Experiments-launched-per-quarter (target: 10+ for top-decile), experiment cycle time (decision-to-rollout), flag adoption rate (% of features behind flags), kill-switch latency (detection-to-rollback), winner-declaration time, statistical-power per experiment (sample size, MDE), false-positive rate.
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: RevenueCat 2026: up to 40x cumulative revenue gap between testing and non-testing teams. Adapty: 636% LTV lift on weekly+3day-trial vs no-trial; 62.3% LTV win rate on localization tests. Statsig: 25%+ annualized revenue lift for active experimenters. Specific-experiment gains 5-30% per test, compounding over time.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI: 14.7+ experiments/yr inferred from variant velocity in Mobbin captures (paywall layouts, plan SKUs, AI-reveal copy iterating weekly). (2) Headspace 2024: public conference talks describe flag-based onboarding experimentation. (3) Headway: visible iteration on TikTok ad creative + onboarding variants (Mobbin). (4) BlueSky onboarding experiments via PostHog (open-source codebase shows posthog.getFeatureFlag calls). (5) Atlassian / IBM / Cisco — LaunchDarkly enterprise customers running flags at scale. (6) Statsig customers: Notion, Brex, Headspace, OpenAI (publicly listed). (7) Adapty's paywall experimentation tooling integrates with PostHog/Mixpanel for cohort analysis. (8) Superwall (paywall-specific tooling) ships A/B test engine. (9) Counter-example failure: many indie subscription apps run zero experiments per year, plateau at $5K MRR. (10) Eppo case study: RetentionEngineering team lifted 30% trial-to-paid via 6 months of flag-based paywall iteration.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-specific challenges: app-launch latency budget for flag fetch (<200ms ideal, fall back to cache); deferred deep-link interaction (assign variant on install vs first-app-open); App Store review variance (don't run experiments during review submission). Web simpler: server-side flag evaluation per request, no app-store cycle. Mobile-specific: SKAdNetwork measurement integration (Apple's privacy-preserving attribution), iOS pre-IDFA cohort analysis. Cross-platform: same flag service, same variant assignment.
fields.category_fit: MANDATORY for: subscription apps with revenue-optimization mandate, high-CAC apps, AI-wrapper / wellness / fintech with funnel-driven business. STRONGLY recommended for: any consumer mobile app with >1K MAU. Skippable for: hobby projects, MVPs pre-launch, B2B with stable enterprise contracts.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. PostHog or Statsig SDK integration (1 day), flag boilerplate + variant components (2 days), first experiment design + launch (1 day), monitoring dashboard (1 day). Solo devs benefit from PostHog free tier (no credit card).
fields.tooling_options: FREE TIER: PostHog (1M events/mo, includes flags + experiments + replay + funnels — best-in-class for solo dev), Statsig (free up to certain volume), GrowthBook (open-source, self-hostable). MID-TIER: ConfigCat ($99-$299/mo), Eppo ($199+/mo), Optimizely ($mid-five-figures+). ENTERPRISE: LaunchDarkly ($30K+/yr), Optimizely, Adobe Target. PAYWALL-SPECIFIC: Adapty (paywall A/B native), Superwall (paywall experimentation), RevenueCat Experiments (free with RevenueCat). REMOTE CONFIG ONLY (no experiments): Firebase Remote Config (free), AWS AppConfig.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to wire flags as a primitive. Prompt fragment 1: 'Integrate PostHog feature flags for onboarding experimentation. (1) Initialize PostHog in app/_layout.tsx with API key from EXPO_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY. (2) Fetch flags at launch via posthog.reloadFeatureFlags(); cache result. (3) Build a useExperiment(experimentKey: string): { variant: string, exposure: () => void } hook that returns the assigned variant and an exposure-tracker (call exposure() exactly once per user-experiment-pair via useEffect). (4) Define experiment keys: onboarding_paywall_layout (control / side_by_side / timeline), onboarding_loader_copy (control / theatrical_v2 / theatrical_v3), onboarding_question_count (control_12 / variant_8 / variant_20), ai_reveal_animation (control / lottie_v2 / skia_particles). (5) Render variant-specific component via switch statement. (6) FORBID: calling posthog.capture without including experiment_variants superproperty. (7) Default to control variant if flag service unavailable. (8) Telemetry: emit experiment_exposure { experiment_key, variant, user_id_hash } once per session.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Pre-register an experiment on PostHog: hypothesis ("Side-by-side paywall layout improves trial-start by 10%"), primary metric (trial_started rate), MDE (10%), guardrail metrics (D7 retention, paywall_dismissed rate), planned sample size (computed via PostHog calculator), runtime (14 days minimum). Output the experiment definition as JSON for the team to review before launch.' Prompt fragment 3: 'Audit the codebase for getFeatureFlag calls — every call must include exposure tracking and variant-specific telemetry. Flag any flag older than 30 days that's at 100% rollout for cleanup.'
fields.stitch_application: N/A operational. Stitch can mockup variants. Prompt fragment: 'Mockup three variants of the onboarding paywall: variant A (control) — toggle paywall in dark mode (forbidden post-2026-01 — flag for compliance), variant B (side-by-side plan selection) — two cards, weekly trial vs annual, badge "most popular" on annual, variant C (timeline transparency) — vertical timeline showing days 1-7 free, day 8 charged. Each in same brand tokens (cream #F4EFE6, ink #1A1816, ember #E36B2C, Cabinet Grotesk + Söhne).'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to hardcoded if/else feature checks (`if (FEATURES.NEW_PAYWALL)`) without runtime evaluation. AI doesn't track exposure events. AI re-evaluates flags on every render (expensive). AI doesn't include experiment_variants in analytics. AI mixes flag boolean (released? on/off) with experiment string (variant assignment) confusingly. Counter-prompt: 'Use PostHog SDK for flags — never hardcoded constants. Implement useExperiment() hook that calls posthog.getFeatureFlag once per mount and tracks exposure once via useEffect. Distinguish RELEASE FLAG (boolean — feature on/off, lifecycle: until full rollout) from EXPERIMENT FLAG (string — variant assignment, lifecycle: until winner declared) from OPS FLAG (kill-switch, permanent). FORBID hardcoded if/else feature checks. ALWAYS include experiment_variants[] superproperty on every analytics event. Cache flag values in memory; refresh on app foreground (not on every render).' AI also peeks at p-values mid-experiment — counter: 'do not look at experiment results before pre-registered sample size; use PostHog sequential-testing if early-stop needed.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Hardcoded if/else feature checks — defeats deploy-decoupling. (2) Forgetting to track exposure — experiment results corrupted. (3) Peeking at p-values mid-experiment — false-positive inflation. (4) Stopping early on positive results — winner's curse. (5) Ignoring guardrail metrics — ship features that improve target metric but tank D7 retention. (6) No flag cleanup — flag debt accumulates, code becomes spaghetti. (7) Multi-variant tests without sample size for each — under-powered. (8) Running 5+ simultaneous experiments on overlapping users — interaction effects, signal dilution. (9) Default-to-broken if flag service down — every flag must have safe default. (10) Same flag controlling unrelated features — bad hygiene. (11) Server-side flags only on mobile — adds 200-500ms launch latency; cache. (12) Not pre-registering hypothesis — post-hoc rationalization. (13) Over-experimentation in early stage (no traffic, every test under-powered) — premature for <1K MAU.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. (a) GDPR Art. 13 transparency — declare experimentation in privacy notice. (b) GDPR consent — EU users must consent to non-essential tracking, including experiments; integrate CMP. (c) DFA Q4 2026 — manipulative-pattern detection; running A/B tests on dark patterns documented as 'optimization' is regulatory-flag worthy. Document experiment governance: ethical review of variants, no testing of confirmed dark patterns. (d) FTC § 5 — A/B testing pricing variants without disclosure has been challenged (e.g., Wells Fargo 2018 personalized-pricing investigation). (e) COPPA — children's data shouldn't enter experiment cohorts without verifiable parental consent. (f) Apple App Store Guideline 4.1 (Copycats / 'meta-rule') — repeated paywall experiments shouldn't drift into deceptive territory.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store: feature flags permitted; flagged features must be 'present at submission' (don't hide major functionality behind flags then enable post-review — Guideline 2.3.1). Google Play: similar guidance. Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (toggle-paywall ban 2026-01) — experiment variants must NOT include forbidden patterns. PrivacyManifest must declare flag/experiment SDKs. SKAdNetwork compatibility for iOS attribution-based experiments.
fields.accessibility_implications: N/A direct. Indirect: experiment guardrails should monitor accessibility metrics (screen-reader-active session completion rate, dynamic-type-large completion rate) to ensure experiments don't degrade a11y experience.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Sample size requirements — small apps under-powered; consider sequential testing or deferring experiments until traffic supports. (2) Multi-armed bandits (Bayesian adaptive) save samples but require care — Statsig / Eppo support. (3) Network effects within experiments (social-graph users) violate independence assumption. (4) Time-varying treatment effects — copy that wins in week 1 may lose by week 4 (novelty fade). (5) Holdout group: keep 5-10% never-treated to measure long-term net effect. (6) Cross-experiment interaction — running paywall test + onboarding test simultaneously confounds; use orthogonal hashes. (7) Mobile platform variance — iOS vs Android may respond differently; analyze by OS. (8) Deep-link entry — variant assigned on install vs first-launch can differ. (9) Re-randomization on app reinstall is a bug — persist variant assignment. (10) Flag fetch latency degrading cold launch — pre-fetch in parallel with splash.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Experimentation is universal; locale interactions important. Adapty 2026: localization tests have 62.3% LTV win rate — locale-stratified experiments are highest-leverage. JP/KR may respond to different paywall framings than US/EU. CN: payment ecosystem differs (WeChat Pay, Alipay) — experiment infrastructure must support. RTL: visual variants must mirror correctly across both treatment and control. Religious / cultural sensitivities: variant copy reviewed per locale before launch (e.g., 'streak-save' guilt copy may offend in some markets).
fields.prescribed_action: Adopt a feature-flag service from Day 1 (PostHog, Statsig, ConfigCat — free tier). Distinguish release flags (boolean), experiment flags (variant string), ops flags (kill-switch). Implement useExperiment() hook with exposure tracking. Pre-register every experiment (hypothesis, MDE, sample size, runtime, guardrails). Run minimum 14 days, never peek mid-experiment. Cleanup flags 30 days post-rollout. Aim for top-decile cadence: 10+ experiments per quarter once traffic supports. Forbid: hardcoded feature checks, untracked exposures, mid-experiment peeking, 5+ simultaneous overlapping experiments.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Indirect — questionnaire variants (number of steps, question types, sequencing) tested via experiment_variant flag. Pair with item 27 to A/B test which question-type-mix performs best.
fields.aha_relevance: Indirect — AI plan reveal (item 12) copy/animation/loader variants are canonical experiment subjects (loader_copy_v1 vs v2 vs v3 — see RevenueCat / Adapty case studies).
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: N/A — but archetype-conditioned variants (item 52) tested via flag (e.g., 'magician_voice' vs 'caregiver_voice' for same product, measure LTV by archetype-fit cohort).
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'no experimentation / annual one-off redesigns' antipattern. Pairs with item 31 (SDUI) — flag determines which payload to fetch. Pairs with item 43 (Tracking Plan) — experiment_variants superproperty. Pairs with item 44 (Funnel + Cohort/LTV) — cohort by experiment_variant.
sources: [Optimizely — Future-Proof Your Mobile App with Feature Flags](https://www.optimizely.com/future-proof-your-mobile-app-with-feature-flags-and-experimentation/)
[ConfigCat — A/B Testing React Native Apps with Feature Flags](https://configcat.com/blog/2025/03/14/ab-testing-react-native-apps-with-feature-flags/)
[Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments — Kohavi, Tang, Xu (Cambridge 2020)](https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/trustworthy-online-controlled-experiments/D97B26382EB0EB2DC2019A7A7B518F59)
uncertain: exact magnitude of 40x revenue gap (RevenueCat citation; methodology not publicly detailed)
